post.description: Create a new feature flag. The caller provides the id (the flag key) in the request body.

get.description:
List feature flags for this account.

Default sort is `key` ascending. ``filter[references_context]`` and
``filter[references_context_type]`` walk the rules JSON in Python after
the SQL fetch, so pagination for those calls is applied in memory after
the filter; for the common case (no rules-traversal filter) pagination
is applied at the SQL level.

parameters.name: filter[references_context]

schema.description: Return flags whose rules reference this context instance. Format: {type}:{key}
schema.title: Filter[References Context]

parameters.description: Return flags whose rules reference this context instance. Format: {type}:{key}

parameters.name: filter[references_context_type]

schema.description: Return flags whose rules reference any attribute of the given context type.
schema.title: Filter[References Context Type]

parameters.description: Return flags whose rules reference any attribute of the given context type.

parameters.name: filter[search]

schema.description: Case-insensitive substring match against the flag `key` and `name`. A flag is returned if either field contains the search term.
schema.title: Filter[Search]

parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match against the flag `key` and `name`. A flag is returned if either field contains the search term.

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Field to sort by. Prefix with `-` for descending order. Default: `key`. Allowed values: `created_at`, `-created_at`, `key`, `-key`, `name`, `-name`, `type`, `-type`, `updated_at`, `-updated_at`.
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Field to sort by. Prefix with `-` for descending order. Default: `key`. Allowed values: `created_at`, `-created_at`, `key`, `-key`, `name`, `-name`, `type`, `-type`, `updated_at`, `-updated_at`.

parameters.name: page[number]

schema.description: 1-based page number to return. Optional; defaults to `1` when omitted. Must be `>= 1` — requests with a smaller value are rejected with a 400 error.
schema.title: Page[Number]

parameters.description: 1-based page number to return. Optional; defaults to `1` when omitted. Must be `>= 1` — requests with a smaller value are rejected with a 400 error.

parameters.name: page[size]

schema.description: Number of items per page. Optional; defaults to `1000` when omitted. Must be between `1` and `1000` inclusive — requests outside that range are rejected with a 400 error.
schema.title: Page[Size]

parameters.description: Number of items per page. Optional; defaults to `1000` when omitted. Must be between `1` and `1000` inclusive — requests outside that range are rejected with a 400 error.

parameters.name: meta[total]

schema.description: When `true`, the response's `meta.pagination` block includes `total` (the total number of matching items across all pages) and `total_pages`. Computing these requires an extra `COUNT` query, so omit (or pass `false`) when the totals are not needed. Defaults to `false`.
schema.title: Meta[Total]

parameters.description: When `true`, the response's `meta.pagination` block includes `total` (the total number of matching items across all pages) and `total_pages`. Computing these requires an extra `COUNT` query, so omit (or pass `false`) when the totals are not needed. Defaults to `false`.

get.description: Retrieve a feature flag by its key.

put.description: Replace a feature flag entirely. Every writable field is overwritten.

delete.description: Delete a feature flag by its key.

post.description:
Register flags discovered by an SDK.

Creates a new flag for each unreported key and refreshes the
service/environment source observation on each already-known key.

get.description:
List the service/environment observations recorded for a single flag.

Default sort is `-last_seen` (most recently seen first).

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Field to sort by. Prefix with `-` for descending order. Default: `-last_seen`. Allowed values: `created_at`, `-created_at`, `environment`, `-environment`, `last_seen`, `-last_seen`, `service`, `-service`.
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Field to sort by. Prefix with `-` for descending order. Default: `-last_seen`. Allowed values: `created_at`, `-created_at`, `environment`, `-environment`, `last_seen`, `-last_seen`, `service`, `-service`.

parameters.name: page[number]

schema.description: 1-based page number to return. Optional; defaults to `1` when omitted. Must be `>= 1` — requests with a smaller value are rejected with a 400 error.
schema.title: Page[Number]

parameters.description: 1-based page number to return. Optional; defaults to `1` when omitted. Must be `>= 1` — requests with a smaller value are rejected with a 400 error.

parameters.name: page[size]

schema.description: Number of items per page. Optional; defaults to `1000` when omitted. Must be between `1` and `1000` inclusive — requests outside that range are rejected with a 400 error.
schema.title: Page[Size]

parameters.description: Number of items per page. Optional; defaults to `1000` when omitted. Must be between `1` and `1000` inclusive — requests outside that range are rejected with a 400 error.

parameters.name: meta[total]

schema.description: When `true`, the response's `meta.pagination` block includes `total` (the total number of matching items across all pages) and `total_pages`. Computing these requires an extra `COUNT` query, so omit (or pass `false`) when the totals are not needed. Defaults to `false`.
schema.title: Meta[Total]

parameters.description: When `true`, the response's `meta.pagination` block includes `total` (the total number of matching items across all pages) and `total_pages`. Computing these requires an extra `COUNT` query, so omit (or pass `false`) when the totals are not needed. Defaults to `false`.

get.description:
List service/environment observations across all flags for this account.

Default sort is `-last_seen` (most recently seen first). Filter by
`environment` or `service` (or both) to narrow the result.

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Field to sort by. Prefix with `-` for descending order. Default: `-last_seen`. Allowed values: `created_at`, `-created_at`, `environment`, `-environment`, `last_seen`, `-last_seen`, `service`, `-service`.
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Field to sort by. Prefix with `-` for descending order. Default: `-last_seen`. Allowed values: `created_at`, `-created_at`, `environment`, `-environment`, `last_seen`, `-last_seen`, `service`, `-service`.

parameters.name: page[number]

schema.description: 1-based page number to return. Optional; defaults to `1` when omitted. Must be `>= 1` — requests with a smaller value are rejected with a 400 error.
schema.title: Page[Number]

parameters.description: 1-based page number to return. Optional; defaults to `1` when omitted. Must be `>= 1` — requests with a smaller value are rejected with a 400 error.

parameters.name: page[size]

schema.description: Number of items per page. Optional; defaults to `1000` when omitted. Must be between `1` and `1000` inclusive — requests outside that range are rejected with a 400 error.
schema.title: Page[Size]

parameters.description: Number of items per page. Optional; defaults to `1000` when omitted. Must be between `1` and `1000` inclusive — requests outside that range are rejected with a 400 error.

parameters.name: meta[total]

schema.description: When `true`, the response's `meta.pagination` block includes `total` (the total number of matching items across all pages) and `total_pages`. Computing these requires an extra `COUNT` query, so omit (or pass `false`) when the totals are not needed. Defaults to `false`.
schema.title: Meta[Total]

parameters.description: When `true`, the response's `meta.pagination` block includes `total` (the total number of matching items across all pages) and `total_pages`. Computing these requires an extra `COUNT` query, so omit (or pass `false`) when the totals are not needed. Defaults to `false`.

post.description:
Remove every rule that references a specific context across every flag.

Provide exactly one of `context` (matches a single instance,
formatted as `{type}:{key}`) or `context_type` (matches any
attribute of that context type). Rules whose reference sits inside
an AND expression are not removed automatically; they are returned
in `rules_needing_manual_review` for the caller to handle.

get.description: Report current-period usage counters for this account.

parameters.name: page[number]

schema.description: 1-based page number to return. Optional; defaults to `1` when omitted. Must be `>= 1` — requests with a smaller value are rejected with a 400 error.
schema.title: Page[Number]

parameters.description: 1-based page number to return. Optional; defaults to `1` when omitted. Must be `>= 1` — requests with a smaller value are rejected with a 400 error.

schema.description: Number of items per page. Optional; defaults to `1000` when omitted. Must be between `1` and `1000` inclusive — requests outside that range are rejected with a 400 error.

200.description: Current usage for the authenticated account

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable display name for the flag. Cannot be empty or whitespace-only.

description.title: Description
description.description: Human-readable description of the flag's purpose.

type.title: Type
type.description: Value type of the flag. Accepted case-insensitively. Changing the type cascades to `values`, `default`, and every environment's rules and default.

default.title: Default
default.description: Default value returned when no environment rule fires and the environment has no `default`. For constrained flags (non-null `values`), must equal one of the entries in the `values` array. For unconstrained flags, must match `type`.

values.title: Values
values.description: Ordered set of allowed values for a constrained flag, or `null` for an unconstrained flag. `BOOLEAN` flags, if constrained, must declare exactly two values.

environments.title: Environments
environments.description: Per-environment configuration keyed by environment name (`production`, `staging`, etc.). Environments not listed fall back to the flag's global `default`.

managed.title: Managed
managed.description: `true` when the flag was created through the API, `false` when it was auto-discovered from a bulk-register call. Auto-discovered flags can be edited and converted to managed by setting this to `true`.

sources.title: Sources
sources.description: SDK-reported observations of this flag, grouped by service and environment. Populated automatically by the bulk-register endpoint.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the flag was last modified.

Flag.title: Flag
Flag.description:
A feature flag whose value is resolved at runtime from environment
rules and a default.

A flag has a value type (`BOOLEAN`, `STRING`, `NUMERIC`, or `JSON`)
and either a fixed set of allowed values (constrained) or accepts
any value matching the type (unconstrained). Each environment can
enable or disable the flag, set its own default, and define
targeting rules that override the default for specific evaluation
contexts.

example.description: Enable dark mode for the application UI
example.name: Dark Mode

id.title: Id
id.description: Flag key as declared in code. URL-safe and stable for the lifetime of the flag.

type.title: Type
type.description: Value type the SDK declared for the flag. Accepted case-insensitively.

default.title: Default
default.description: Default value the SDK declared for the flag. Used to create the flag if it does not already exist.

service.title: Service
service.description: Service reporting the declaration. Defaults to `unknown`.

environment.title: Environment
environment.description: Environment reporting the declaration. Defaults to `unknown`.

FlagBulkItem.title: FlagBulkItem
FlagBulkItem.description: One flag declaration reported by an SDK during bulk registration.

flags.title: Flags
flags.description: Flags reported by the SDK in this batch.

registered.title: Registered
registered.description: Number of items in the batch that were registered or refreshed.

FlagCreateRequest.title: FlagCreateRequest
FlagCreateRequest.description:
JSON:API request envelope for creating a flag.

Distinct from :class:`FlagRequest` because create requires
caller-supplied ``data.id`` while update does not.

FlagCreateResource.title: FlagCreateResource
FlagCreateResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for creating a flag (id required).

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Whether the flag is active in this environment. When `false`, evaluation skips rules and returns the flag's global `default`.

default.title: Default
default.description: Environment-level default returned when no rule fires. If `null`, evaluation falls back to the flag's global `default`.

rules.title: Rules
rules.description: Targeting rules evaluated top-down. The first rule whose logic returns truthy provides the result.

FlagEnvironment.title: FlagEnvironment
FlagEnvironment.description: Per-environment evaluation configuration for a flag.

FlagListResponse.title: FlagListResponse
FlagListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response envelope for flags.

FlagRequest.title: FlagRequest
FlagRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for updating a flag.

FlagResource.title: FlagResource
FlagResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for a flag.

`id` is the flag key. For create requests, `id` is required and is
chosen by the caller. For update requests, `id` may be omitted (the
server reads the key from the URL) or supplied to rename the flag.

attributes.description: Enable dark mode for the application UI
attributes.name: Dark Mode

FlagResponse.title: FlagResponse
FlagResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for a flag.

logic.title: Logic
logic.description: JSON Logic expression evaluated against the evaluation context. The rule fires when the expression is truthy.

value.title: Value
value.description: Value returned when the rule fires. Must reference a value from the flag's `values` array (constrained flags) or match the flag's `type` (unconstrained flags).

FlagRule.title: FlagRule
FlagRule.description: A targeting rule that overrides the default within an environment.

environment.title: Environment
environment.description: Environment in which the service declared the flag.

declared_type.title: Declared Type
declared_type.description: Value type the SDK reported when registering the flag from this service/environment. May differ from the flag's authoritative `type` if the service is running stale code.

declared_default.title: Declared Default
declared_default.description: Default value the SDK reported when registering the flag from this service/environment. May differ from the flag's authoritative `default` if the service is running stale code.

first_observed.title: First Observed
first_observed.description: When this source was first observed.

last_seen.title: Last Seen
last_seen.description: Most recent time the SDK re-registered this source.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: When the source record was created.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the source record was last modified.

FlagSource.title: FlagSource
FlagSource.description:
A record of an SDK observing a feature flag from a particular
service and environment.

The flags service auto-registers a source the first time an SDK
reports a flag from a given service/environment pair and refreshes
`last_seen` on every subsequent report. Each source captures the
value type and default value the SDK declared in source code at
that location, which makes it possible to detect when service code
has drifted from the flag's authoritative configuration.

FlagSourceListResponse.title: FlagSourceListResponse
FlagSourceListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response envelope for flag sources.

FlagSourceResource.title: FlagSourceResource
FlagSourceResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for a flag source.

`id` is the source record's UUID. Sources are not created or
modified directly — the flags service registers and refreshes them
in response to SDK bulk-register requests.

value.title: Value
value.description: The value itself. Must match the flag's `type`.

FlagValue.title: FlagValue
FlagValue.description: A named value in a constrained flag's value set.

ListMeta.title: ListMeta
ListMeta.description: Top-level ``meta`` block included on every JSON:API list response.

flag.title: Flag
flag.description: Key of the flag containing the rule.

rule_index.title: Rule Index
rule_index.description: Position of the rule within the environment's `rules` array.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Why the rule needs manual review.

ManualReviewItem.title: ManualReviewItem
ManualReviewItem.description:
A flag rule that could not be safely modified by the bulk
remove-references action.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of matching items across all pages. Present only when the request included `meta[total]=true`.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page size. Present only when the request included `meta[total]=true`.

PaginationMeta.title: PaginationMeta
PaginationMeta.description:
Pagination block returned inside ``meta`` on every list response.

``page`` and ``size`` are always present and echo the parameters that
served the response (their defaults when the client omitted them).
``total`` and ``total_pages`` are present only when the request included
``meta[total]=true``.

flags_modified.title: Flags Modified
flags_modified.description: Keys of flags whose rules were modified.

rules_removed.title: Rules Removed
rules_removed.description: Total number of rules removed across all flags.

rules_needing_manual_review.title: Rules Needing Manual Review
rules_needing_manual_review.description: Rules that referenced the context but could not be removed automatically (typically because the reference is inside an `and` expression where removal would broaden the rule).

RemoveReferencesAttributes.title: RemoveReferencesAttributes
RemoveReferencesAttributes.description: Counts and follow-ups returned by the remove-references action.

context.title: Context
context.description: Identifier of the context instance to remove references to, formatted as `{type}:{key}` (e.g. `customer:c-123`).

context_type.title: Context Type
context_type.description: Context type to remove all references to (any attribute of this type).

RemoveReferencesRequest.title: RemoveReferencesRequest
RemoveReferencesRequest.description:
Inputs to the remove-references action.

Exactly one of `context` or `context_type` must be provided.

RemoveReferencesResultEnvelope.title: RemoveReferencesResultEnvelope
RemoveReferencesResultEnvelope.description:
JSON:API single-resource response envelope for the
remove-references action.

RemoveReferencesResultResource.title: RemoveReferencesResultResource
RemoveReferencesResultResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for the remove-references result.

rules_needing_manual_review.reason: Context reference inside an AND expression — removing would broaden the rule

limit_key.title: Limit Key
limit_key.description: Identifier of the metered limit, e.g. `flags.managed_flags`.

period.title: Period
period.description: Period the counter covers. `current` is the only supported value.

UsageAttributes.title: UsageAttributes
UsageAttributes.description: Usage counter for a single metered limit.

UsageListResponse.title: UsageListResponse
UsageListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response envelope for usage counters.

UsageResource.title: UsageResource
UsageResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for a usage counter.
